aliases: the sleep cycle of agent memory
attach synthesis cluster to topic graph
blurb: The offline sleep cycle: how working rows are deduped, promoted, decayed, synthesized, and retired.
intent: Explain `reflect()` — the batch consolidation cycle that turns raw `working` rows into curated `episodic` knowledge — step by ordered step: near-duplicate dedup (`_dedup_and_judge`, embedding cosine or text-ratio fallback), LLM-judged contradiction in the similarity gray zone, working→episodic promotion with `recall_count`-driven importance (`_promote`), forget-stale retirement of never-recalled aged rows (`_forget_stale`), a pre-decay engagement densifying sweep (`_score_pending`), importance decay for chronically-ignored rows whose gate reads the soft/hard engagement split (`_decay_chronically_ignored`/`_decay_reason`), stale-reference flagging with git rename-follow (`_flag_stale_references`), LLM synthesis of higher-order rules from cosine clusters (`_synthesize`), an optional per-scope digest (`_synthesize_digest`), content-hash-skipped embedding (`_embed_episodic`), and the `related` edge harvest (`_harvest_edges`). `topic_attach.py` proposes merge/create onto the human-approved `.regin/topics` graph.
label: Memory consolidation (reflect: dedup, contradiction, promote, synthesize, forget-stale, decay, edges & topics)
